item.title: Vance, Bessent questioned tech giants on AI security before Anthropic's Mythos release, CNBC reports
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance and Treasury ​Secretary Scott Bessent questioned leading tech ‌CEOs about AI model security and how to respond to cyber attacks a week before Anthropic ​released its new Mythos model, ​CNBC reported on Friday.

item.title: Rio Tinto's California boron assets draw interest from bidders, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Rio Tinto's , U.S. assets that produce the critical mineral boron have drawn interest from ​more than a dozen potential bidders and could ‌be valued as much as $2 billion, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing sources.

item.title: Proxy adviser Glass Lewis recommends Warner Bros shareholders vote for Paramount deal
item.description: Independent proxy advisory firm Glass Lewis recommended Warner Bros Discovery shareholders to vote in favor of the company's $110 billion deal to ​combine with Paramount Skydance .

item.title: Americans give record-low marks to economy, in ominous sign for Republicans
item.description: Americans are souring on the U.S. economy to a degree never before seen, with the stiffest inflation in four ​years exacerbating the political risk for President Donald Trump, who some administration officials worry has lost focus on affordability problems for voters as he ‌trains attention on the war against Iran.

item.title: Middle East war to cut growth, deliver cascading impact, World Bank chief says
item.description: The war in ​the Middle East will have a cascading impact on the global economy, ‌even if the fragile ceasefire announced by U.S. President Donald Trump takes hold, World Bank President Ajay Banga told Reuters in an interview on Friday.

item.title: Suspect arrested after Molotov cocktail attack at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home
item.description: A person was arrested by ​San Francisco police for ‌allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at ​OpenAI CEO Sam ​Altman's home and for ⁠making threats outside ​the artificial intelligence ​startup's headquarters, the company said on Friday.

item.title: U.S. Democrats keep pressure on Trump regulators over 'suspicious' trades
item.description: Two Democratic U.S. senators have called on the federal commodities markets watchdog to investigate lucrative recent ​trading activity on oil markets since March ‌23 that closely coincided with White House moves in the Iran war, adding to pressure from opposition party members on ​possible insider trading, according to a letter released ​Friday.

item.title: USA Rare Earth CEO defends Trump administration investment as congressional questions swirl
item.description: USA Rare Earth shareholders should not be concerned by the terms of the U.S. Commerce Department's pending investment in the company, which gives equity to ​Washington even if funding for the deal falls apart, CEO Barbara Humpton told Reuters.

item.title: Oil whiplash: Iran war shock to flip market to deficit in 2026, analysts say
item.description: The sharp hit to global oil production from the Iran war is poised ​to flip the oil market into a supply deficit this year, analysts say, a huge swing in forecasts that erases ‌previous expectations of comfortable oversupply.

item.title: Fed will need to explain why current inflation jump differs from 2022 surge
item.description: When surging U.S. inflation peaked in June of 2022, driven by rising prices for energy but also for food, shelter, vehicles, and a host of other items, Federal Reserve officials assured the public in stern tones ​that they were on top of the situation and would follow through with steep interest rate hikes to cool the economy.

item.title: US March budget deficit rises slightly to $164 billion, war outlays delayed
item.description: The U.S. federal government reported that the March budget deficit rose $4 billion or 2% ​to $164 billion from a year ago as ‌new individual and corporate tax breaks pushed refunds sharply higher, while relief payments to farmers also grew, the ​U.S. Treasury said on Friday.

item.title: South Africa unveils draft AI policy, proposes new institutions and incentives
item.description: South Africa on Friday unveiled a draft national AI policy, seeking public comment on sweeping proposals to regulate and accelerate AI adoption.

item.title: Exclusive: Commvault explores sale after takeover interest, sources say
item.description: Data protection software provider Commvault Systems ‌is exploring a sale after receiving takeover interest from multiple parties, according to four people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Wall St Week Ahead US earnings season set to test war-rattled stocks
item.description: Investors will seek evidence in the coming week that ​the U.S. corporate profit engine is humming along, and whether threats to that upbeat business outlook are emerging from the Middle East war and the resulting ‌surge in energy costs.

item.title: Whirlpool to invest over $60 million in Ohio plant
item.description: Whirlpool said on Friday it will invest over $60 ​million in an Ohio factory to ‌produce parts for its washers and dryers.

item.title: What the Trump tax breaks giveth, the gasoline pump taketh away
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump in February touted this year's tax refunds as "substantially greater than ever before," thanks to new individual tax breaks approved in 2025, telling taxpayers that they should think of him when the funds hit their bank accounts.

item.title: US judge extends temporary order halting Nexstar, Tegna merger
item.description: A U.S. judge on ​Friday extended an ‌order temporarily freezing Nexstar's acquisition of rival ​broadcast station owner ​Tegna for another week ⁠as he ​decides whether to issue ​a preliminary injunction.

item.title: YouTube raises subscription prices in US for the first time in 3 years
item.description: YouTube on Friday raised subscription prices in the United States, with increases of up to $4 set to ​take effect from the next billing cycle.

item.title: Outflows from leveraged loan funds jump as investors shun credit risk
item.description: U.S. leveraged loan funds are suffering heavy outflows this year as investors grow more cautious about credit risk while broader concerns mount over ​redemption pressures and limited transparency in private credit markets.

item.title: Ackman's $64 billion Universal bet hinges on power broker Bollore
item.description: Bill Ackman's first call before launching his $64 billion bid for Universal Music Group this week was to French billionaire Vincent Bollore, the U.S. hedge fund manager said.

item.title: Exclusive: Impactive aims to replace WEX CEO on board in proxy fight, sources say
item.description: Impactive Capital is urging WEX shareholders to replace the company's long-serving CEO on ​its board of directors at next month's annual meeting as the activist investor presses on with a board ‌challenge, according to sources and documents seen by Reuters.

item.title: US factory orders unchanged in February for second straight month
item.description: New orders for U.S. factory goods were unchanged for a second straight month ​in February as weak demand for commercial ‌aircraft offset gains elsewhere.

item.title: OpenAI faces tighter regulation under EU's Digital Service Act, Handelsblatt says
item.description: OpenAI and its chatbot ​ChatGPT is set ‌to be classified as a very ​large search ​engine and fall under ⁠the European ​Union's Digital Services ​Act (DSA), meaning it faces tighter regulation, Germany's Handelsblatt ​newspaper said ​on Friday, citing sources.

item.title: US consumer sentiment dives to a record low in April amid Iran war
item.description: U.S. consumer sentiment plunged to a record low in early April and consumers anticipated a ​surge in inflation in the next 12 ‌months, a survey showed on Friday.

item.title: Iran war leaves crisis-scarred countries counting the cost
item.description: Sanoj Weeratunge thought this would finally be the year his tour firm put Sri ​Lanka's spate of crises behind it. Then the Iran war erupted 2,700 miles away, the government hiked fuel prices by 35% and business slumped almost a third.

item.title: Meta must face youth addiction lawsuit by Massachusetts, court rules
item.description: Meta Platforms must face a lawsuit by Massachusetts' attorney general alleging that the Facebook and Instagram parent deliberately designed features to addict young users, the state's top court ruled on Friday.

item.title: Exclusive: Swiss bank Julius Baer initiates search to replace its CFO, sources say
item.description: Julius Baer is considering replacing its ​chief financial officer, two people ‌familiar with discussions said, a step that would round ​off a shake-up in ​the private bank's top ⁠management since it racked ​up heavy losses linked to ​risky lending.

item.title: Lockheed Martin secures $4.7 billion Patriot missile interceptor production contract
item.description: Lockheed Martin said on ​Friday the U.S. government ‌awarded the defense giant a $4.7 billion preliminary contract to ​continue critical accelerated production ​of PAC-3 Missile Segment Enhancement.

item.title: VIEW March inflation report, first to show impact of war, is in line with expectations
item.description: U.S. consumer prices rose 3.3% from a year ago in March, data showed, giving investors a first glimpse of the impact on prices of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.

item.title: CoreWeave strikes AI cloud deal with Anthropic, shares rise
item.description: Cloud infrastructure firm CoreWeave said on Friday it has struck a deal with Anthropic to supply the AI startup ​with cloud computing capacity, sending its shares up ‌more than 5% in premarket trading.
